text: Fluorescence angiography is performed during a transanal transtomal proctectomy and ileal pouch anal anastomosis. The surgeon mobilizes the stoma and resects the iliocutaneous suture using a linear stapler. The last ileal loop is returned into the abdomen, and neomarconium is created with a single-port device. Adhesions from previous surgery are lysed to improve bowel mobility. The mesenteric pedicle is prepared and exposed, isolating the mesenteric axis at its origin. The ileal loop is stretched toward the pelvis, and the Cochrane maneuver is performed to increase the reach of the future pouch.

The surgeon exteriorizes the last ileal loop designated for pouch construction. Using transillumination, ileocolic vessels are identified and clamped at their origin. Indocyanine green is injected intravenously, and real-time angiography is performed to assess bowel perfusion. If additional length is required, the ileocolic vessels are sectioned after confirming adequate residual perfusion with fluorescence. The pouch reach is assessed using the inferior aspect of the symphysis pubis. The pouch is constructed with two fires of a 100mm linear stapler, and its patency is confirmed by inflating 300cc of saline solution. Vascularization is reassessed post-construction. A purse string suture is created at the apex of the pouch using Prolene Zero, and a 31mm anvil is inserted. A 19 French Redon drain is placed at the tip of the anvil.

Two surgical teams perform a simultaneous transanal and transtomal proctectomy. An anal retractor is placed, and a pudendal block is administered with a long-acting anesthetic. A purse-string suture is created 3 cm from the dentate line using a circular anal dilator. Transabdominally, the rectum is mobilized, and the inferior mesenteric pedicle is isolated. A stable pneumo-rectum is maintained using a single-axis transanal device and an insulator-smoke evacuator. Circumferential rectotomy is completed.

The transanal team initiates a total mesorectal excision (TME) to minimize nerve damage, while the abdominal team sections the inferior mesenteric vessels. Transillumination is used to connect the superior and inferior dissection planes. The transabdominal dissection continues until it meets the transanal dissection, completing the full mobilization of the rectum.

The surgical specimen is extracted through the transtomal single-port device. A laparoscopic grasper is used to pull the tubular drain at the tip of the anvil, transposing the ileal pouch into the pelvic cavity. The surgeon checks the correct orientation of the pouch and places a purse-string suture at the distal rectal cuff transanally. The anvil is pulled through the distal rectal cuff, and the distal purse-string suture is tied around the anvil before creating the anastomosis. The ileal pouch-anal anastomosis is completed using a 31mm silk cord stapler, and the integrity of the donuts is checked. The anastomosis is evaluated transanally using fluorescence angiography. A diverting ileostomy is created.
